description: Use of eval() can execute untrusted input as code, enabling arbitrary code execution.
fix: Avoid eval(). Use safe parsing and strict input validation, or replace with explicit logic.

description: Hardcoded credentials can leak through source control and be abused by attackers.
fix: Move credentials to a secure secret manager or environment variables and rotate exposed secrets.

description: API keys or tokens hardcoded in source can be extracted and abused.
fix: Store secrets in environment variables, vaults, or secret management systems.

description: exec() function can execute arbitrary system commands if inputs are not sanitized.
fix: Use parameterized execution or libraries that prevent command injection.

description: String concatenation in SQL queries can enable SQL injection attacks.
fix: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries (e.g., placeholders).

description: String concatenation with quotes suggests potential SQL injection vulnerability.
fix: Replace with parameterized queries and prepared statements.

description: Unfiltered SELECT queries may indicate dynamic SQL construction.
fix: Use ORM or parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.

description: Direct innerHTML assignment can lead to DOM-based XSS if user input is not sanitized.
fix: Use textContent or DOM APIs instead; sanitize HTML with libraries like DOMPurify.

description: React's dangerouslySetInnerHTML bypasses XSS protections; risky if data is untrusted.
fix: Avoid this API; sanitize HTML with DOMPurify or use safe alternatives.

description: If JWT verification is misconfigured, it can be bypassed.
fix: Ensure algorithms are explicitly specified and 'none' algorithm is disabled.

description: If randomBytes is misused or insufficient entropy, security is compromised.
fix: Use adequate entropy; crypto.randomBytes(32+) for tokens.

description: MD5 is cryptographically broken and should not be used for security.
fix: Use SHA-256 or bcrypt for password hashing, SHA-256+ for general hashing.

description: SHA-1 is deprecated; collision attacks are feasible.
fix: Migrate to SHA-256 or stronger algorithms.

description: Accessing environment variables can leak sensitive data if misconfigured.
fix: Validate and sanitize environment variables; use secret management tools.

description: Direct manipulation of require.cache can introduce unexpected behavior.
fix: Avoid modifying require.cache; use proper module management.

description: Assigning to global scope can cause namespace pollution and conflicts.
fix: Use local variables or module-scoped variables instead.

description: TODO comments indicate unfinished work that may have security implications.
fix: Complete the implementation or add a security review ticket.

description: FIXME comments indicate acknowledged bugs or issues.
fix: Address the issue or document why it cannot be fixed.

description: URLs with embedded passwords (user:password@host) leak credentials.
fix: Move credentials to environment variables or secure storage.

description: Unvalidated children in React/Vue components can introduce XSS.
fix: Validate and sanitize children if they contain user input.

description: execSync can execute shell commands and is dangerous with untrusted input.
fix: Use spawn/execFile with explicit arguments and strict input validation.

description: Function constructor executes dynamically generated code, similar to eval.
fix: Avoid dynamic code generation and use explicit logic paths.

description: Python pickle deserialization can execute arbitrary code if input is untrusted.
fix: Avoid pickle for untrusted data; use json or a safer serialization format.

description: yaml.load may deserialize arbitrary objects depending on loader configuration.
fix: Use safe_load/safe parser modes and never parse untrusted YAML unsafely.

description: Java Runtime.exec can execute shell/system commands from untrusted sources.
fix: Avoid direct command execution or strictly validate and parameterize inputs.

description: Disabling certificate validation enables man-in-the-middle attacks.
fix: Enable strict TLS verification and use trusted CA certificates.

description: Requests configured with verify=false skip TLS certificate checks.
fix: Set verify to true and use valid certificate trust chains.

description: Plain HTTP transmits data unencrypted and is vulnerable to interception.
fix: Use HTTPS endpoints and enforce TLS for all external communication.

description: Basic auth may expose credentials if transport/security controls are weak.
fix: Use stronger auth mechanisms (OAuth/JWT/mTLS) with secure transport.

description: A GitHub personal access token pattern appears hardcoded in source.
fix: Revoke the token immediately, rotate secrets, and move tokens to secure secret storage.

description: A possible AWS access key appears in source code.
fix: Deactivate leaked keys and store credentials in a secret manager/role-based auth.

description: A provider secret key pattern appears hardcoded in source.
fix: Rotate the key and move it to environment-managed secrets.
